tagline: Automated direct mail for Shopify covering acquisition, retargeting, and retention

pricingIntel.summary: As low as $0.50 per card including postage. No monthly platform fee visible
details: No visible monthly subscription or platform fee
Pricing likely scales with volume and card format (postcard vs. catalog)
Full-service option available (strategy, creative, execution, optimization)
costDrivers: Volume of cards mailed. cost is per-piece, so total scales linearly
Card format. postcards vs. letters vs. catalogs at different price points
Targeting scope. larger audience segments = more cards = higher cost
Managed service. hands-off execution may carry a premium

benefits.label: Lapsed customer reactivation
benefits.description: Direct mail reaches customers who've stopped opening emails. A physical card in the mailbox cuts through digital fatigue and brings back lapsed buyers.

repeatRate.label: Average orders per customer per year

churnRate.label: % of customers who lapse annually
churnRate.source: dtcmvp estimate (30-50% annual churn typical for DTC)

reactivationRate.label: % of mailed lapsed customers who purchase
reactivationRate.source: dtcmvp estimate (PostPilot case studies show 1.2-6% conversion, we SWAG 3%)

benefits.label: New customer acquisition via direct mail
benefits.description: PostPilot's AcquisitionAI targets lookalike prospects with postcards. Case studies show 3.8-11.5x ROAS on acquisition campaigns.

monthlyMailBudget.label: Monthly direct mail spend on acquisition
monthlyMailBudget.source: dtcmvp estimate ($1k-5k/month typical for mid-size DTC acquisition campaigns)

acquisitionRoas.label: ROAS on acquisition direct mail
acquisitionRoas.source: dtcmvp estimate (PostPilot claims 3.8-11.5x across case studies, we SWAG 4x conservatively)

benefits.label: Revenue from email-unreachable customers
benefits.description: 30-50% of your email list has stopped opening. Direct mail is the only channel that reaches them physically. This is revenue you cannot capture digitally.

emailUnreachablePct.label: % of email list that no longer opens emails
emailUnreachablePct.source: industry avg (email open rates ~40-50%, meaning 50-60% don't open regularly, 30-40% are effectively unreachable)

mailConversionRate.label: % of mailed email-unreachable contacts who purchase
mailConversionRate.source: dtcmvp estimate (direct mail conversion: 1-4%, conservative for re-engagement)

sources: Dr. Squatch case study: 10X+ ROAS, 6% conversion rate
PostPilot claims: 'As low as $.50 per card, including postage'
User testimonial: 'Our first campaign we saw a 1000% ROI'

narrative.headline: PostPilot could add {totalValue}/yr to {brandName} through direct mail.

byDepartment.Retention / CRM: For {contactName} on the retention side: PostPilot reaches the 30-50% of your email list that has stopped opening. Direct mail reactivates lapsed customers that no digital channel can touch. The {benefit_lapsed_reactivation}/yr in reactivation revenue comes from customers {brandName} has already paid to acquire.
byDepartment.Marketing / Growth: For {contactName} and the marketing team: direct mail is the channel your competitors are not using. PostPilot's acquisition campaigns deliver 3.8-11.5x ROAS based on case studies, and the reactivation play brings back customers that email has lost for {brandName}.
byDepartment.Performance / Paid: For {contactName} on the paid side: direct mail acquisition through PostPilot gives {brandName} a physical channel with measurable ROAS. At our conservative 4x SWAG, {benefit_acquisition_roas}/yr in acquisition revenue complements your digital spend.
byDepartment.Finance / Accounting: For {contactName} reviewing the budget: PostPilot is a per-card cost model (starting at $0.50/card), not a monthly SaaS fee. {brandName}'s total spend scales with volume mailed. At {targetMultiple} target, the ceiling is {maxMonthly}/mo across all direct mail programs.
byDepartment.Founder & CEO: For {contactName}: PostPilot adds {totalValue}/yr to {brandName} through lapsed customer reactivation, new customer acquisition, and reaching email-unreachable contacts. At your {targetMultiple} target, the ceiling is {maxMonthly}/mo.
byDepartment.Other: PostPilot adds {totalValue}/yr to {brandName} by reaching customers that email cannot: lapsed buyers, email-fatigued contacts, and new lookalike prospects through physical mail.

byCategory.Apparel & Fashion: Apparel brands see strong direct mail performance because fashion is visual and seasonal. A well-timed postcard featuring new arrivals or a VIP discount drives {brandName}'s lapsed customers back at higher conversion rates than digital retargeting.
byCategory.Beauty & Cosmetics: Beauty brands benefit from direct mail for VIP retention and winback campaigns. A physical card with a personalized offer feels premium in a way that email cannot match, which aligns with how beauty customers expect to be treated.
byCategory.Home & Electronics: Home and electronics brands see high AOV on direct mail conversions because these are considered purchases. A postcard that arrives at the right moment can tip a buyer who has been browsing but has not committed online.
byCategory.Other: Direct mail performance varies by category, but the core insight is universal: 30-50% of your email list is unreachable digitally. Physical mail is the only channel that gets to them.

notes: PostPilot's cost model is per-card, not a monthly SaaS fee. Total cost scales with volume mailed. The lapsed customer benefit is the strongest starting point for most brands. it targets a segment that email literally cannot reach. The acquisition benefit requires a monthly mail budget commitment.
